Appointments.reason: Lumbar Strain Due to Postural Overuse
Stable Angina – Follow-Up After Initial Diagnosis
Follow-up for Post-Operative Knee Arthroscopy Recovery
Follow-up for Viral Fever and Sore Throat in Child

Consultations.notes: Patient presents with elevated blood pressure readings. Lifestyle modifications recommended along with medication.

prescriptions.instructions: Take with food, monitor blood pressure

Consultations.diagnosis: Soft tissue injury (Grade I strain) – healing well with conservative management
Consultations.notes: Patient reports improvement in symptoms since the last visit. Pain is now occasional and manageable without medication. Mild stiffness remains, particularly in the morning. No signs of infection or worsening. The patient has adhered well to rest and physiotherapy instructions. On examination, mild swelling is still present, but range of motion has improved. No new injuries noted. Recommend continuing conservative care and beginning light exercise to restore function.

prescriptions.instructions: Take only if pain returns or becomes bothersome. Discontinue if no pain is experienced.

symptoms: Localized pain in the lower back
Consultations.diagnosis: Lumbar strain – likely due to poor posture and overuse. No neurological signs. Conservative treatment advised.
Consultations.notes: Patient complains of lower back pain that has developed gradually over the last week. The pain is dull, increases after prolonged sitting, and is relieved by movement. No radiating pain or numbness. Physical exam reveals tightness in lumbar paraspinal muscles and mild tenderness over L3-L5 region. No red flags observed. Advised posture correction, ergonomic adjustments, and physiotherapy focusing on core strengthening and stretching.

prescriptions.instructions: Take after meals; continue only if pain persists beyond 3 days.

symptoms: Knee pain during stair climbing and squatting
Consultations.diagnosis: Patellofemoral pain syndrome (Runner’s knee) – overuse injury common in active individuals.
Consultations.notes: The patient is physically active and reports anterior knee pain aggravated by stairs, squats, and prolonged sitting. No history of trauma. On examination, there is slight patellar maltracking and tenderness around the patella. No swelling or effusion. Functional tests indicate weak quadriceps and tight hamstrings. Diagnosed with patellofemoral pain syndrome. Advised activity modification, quadriceps strengthening, and hamstring stretching exercises. No surgical intervention required.

prescriptions.instructions: Take with food; avoid if gastric discomfort occurs.

symptoms: reduced strength in the affected limb
Consultations.diagnosis: Healing distal radius fracture – stable alignment, good callus formation, early functional recovery observed.
Consultations.notes: The patient is in the recovery phase post-cast removal for a distal radius fracture sustained 6 weeks ago. They report reduced pain but some weakness and stiffness remain. No signs of neurovascular compromise. On examination, there is mild tenderness at the fracture site and limited grip strength, but range of motion is improving. Patient is advised to continue physiotherapy focused on strength and flexibility. X-ray confirms proper healing. No complications noted.

prescriptions.instructions: Continue supplementation to support bone healing.

symptoms: primarily on the forehead and cheeks.
Consultations.diagnosis: Mild to moderate Acne Vulgaris – comedonal and inflammatory type.
Consultations.notes: Patient presents with a 3-month history of persistent acne, worsened recently due to stress and possible dietary triggers. Lesions are most prominent on the forehead and upper cheeks, with a mix of open and closed comedones, along with a few pustules. No scarring or nodules observed at this stage. Patient reports no prior treatment. Skin type is oily. Emphasized importance of consistent skincare routine and avoiding squeezing pimples. Advised on gentle cleansing and non-comedogenic products. Follow-up scheduled in 4 weeks to monitor response.

prescriptions.dosage: Apply thin layer to affected areas
prescriptions.instructions: Start every other night to reduce irritation; avoid eye and mouth areas.

Consultations.notes: Patient is a known case of hypertension, diagnosed 6 months ago. Currently on regular antihypertensive therapy. Reports good medication adherence with occasional missed doses. BP on examination: 132/86 mmHg. Pulse: 78 bpm, regular. No abnormal heart sounds or peripheral edema. Patient denies any recent episodes of chest discomfort or palpitations. Lifestyle changes have been moderately followed, with some improvement in diet and reduced salt intake. Advised to continue monitoring BP at home and encouraged further weight management and physical activity.

prescriptions.instructions: Take at the same time daily, preferably with breakfast. Monitor blood pressure weekly.
Take after dinner. Recheck lipid profile in 3 months.

Consultations.diagnosis: Stable Angina (NYHA Class II) – Under Medical Management
Consultations.notes: Patient presents for follow-up after being diagnosed with stable angina 4 weeks ago. Reports chest discomfort when climbing stairs or walking briskly, which resolves with rest. No episodes at rest or during sleep. Adherence to medication has been good. ECG shows no new changes; vitals are stable. Pulse: 74 bpm, BP: 128/80 mmHg. Weight slightly reduced since last visit. No signs of fluid overload or heart failure. Advised to continue medications, monitor symptoms, and avoid physical overexertion. Referred for a stress test in the coming week to assess exercise tolerance.

prescriptions.instructions: Place under the tongue at onset of chest pain. Repeat once after 5 minutes if pain persists, up to 3 doses. Seek immediate care if no relief.
Take with meals. Do not skip or double dose.
Take after breakfast to minimize gastric irritation.

Consultations.notes: Patient presents with a first-time episode of a severe, unilateral throbbing headache accompanied by nausea, photophobia, and visual aura. The episode lasted around 6 hours and resolved spontaneously after rest in a dark room. No prior history of similar symptoms. No neurological deficits on examination. No family history of migraines or seizures. Brain imaging is not warranted at this stage due to normal clinical exam and classic presentation. Educated patient about migraine triggers and non-pharmacological management. Prescribed abortive medication and advised to maintain a headache diary.

prescriptions.duration: As needed, maximum 2 tablets in 24 hours
prescriptions.instructions: Take at the first sign of migraine. Avoid driving after taking. Do not exceed the dose.
Use for mild headache or fever. Do not exceed 8 tablets/day.

symptoms: no chest pain or shortness of breath
Consultations.diagnosis: Essential Hypertension – Suboptimal Control on Current Regimen
Consultations.notes: Patient reports mild morning headaches and occasional light-headedness. No recent medication changes. Blood pressure today is 152/96 mmHg, slightly elevated despite adherence to current antihypertensive therapy. No signs of hypertensive retinopathy or cardiac complications. Physical exam is otherwise unremarkable. Discussed lifestyle modifications and importance of salt restriction and weight management. Current medication regimen appears insufficient for optimal BP control. Plan to adjust medications and monitor closely.

prescriptions.instructions: Take at the same time each day. Avoid potassium-rich foods or supplements.

Consultations.notes: Patient reports a long-standing history of dull, non-throbbing headaches occurring bilaterally with a sensation of tightness or pressure. Symptoms are more frequent during workdays and periods of emotional stress. No associated nausea, vomiting, photophobia, or aura. Headaches are relieved with rest and over-the-counter analgesics. Neurological examination is normal. No red flag symptoms to suggest secondary causes. Patient was counseled on lifestyle changes, stress management techniques, posture correction, and regular exercise. Non-pharmacological management emphasized.

prescriptions.instructions: Take daily at bedtime to reduce headache frequency. May cause drowsiness.

Consultations.diagnosis: Post-operative recovery following arthroscopic meniscectomy – healing as expected
Consultations.notes: Patient is 4 weeks post knee arthroscopy for medial meniscus tear. Reports progressive improvement in mobility. Occasional clicking sounds noted during flexion but no locking or instability. Pain has reduced significantly and is mostly activity-related. Slight stiffness experienced after sitting for long durations. Gait shows mild limp, improving with physiotherapy. Wound site healed with no signs of infection. Advised to continue strengthening exercises and increase activity level gradually.

prescriptions.instructions: Take after meals to relieve residual pain during activity.

symptoms: dry patches on elbows and behind knees
Consultations.notes: Patient reports intermittent flare-ups of eczema, primarily during weather changes or after exposure to dust. The current flare is mild with localized dryness and itching. Skin examination shows dry, scaly patches on flexural areas (elbows and behind knees), with no signs of infection or weeping lesions. The patient has been compliant with emollient use but inconsistently applies topical medication during flare-ups. Educated on importance of regular moisturization and avoiding known triggers (wool, harsh soaps, dust). Emphasis placed on routine skin care maintenance to prevent recurrence.

prescriptions.instructions: Use during flare-up only. Avoid prolonged use.

Consultations.notes: Child presented with a 3-day history of low-grade fever and sore throat. Parent reports improvement in fever after administering paracetamol. Cough is mild, mostly dry. Appetite is slightly reduced, but hydration is adequate. On examination, throat appears erythematous with no pus or swelling. Chest is clear on auscultation, and there are no signs of ear infection. Vitals are stable. Diagnosis is consistent with a viral infection. No need for antibiotics at this stage. Advised supportive care and monitoring for worsening symptoms.

prescriptions.frequency: Every 6–8 hours as needed for fever
prescriptions.instructions: Administer only if temperature is above 100°F. Maintain hydration.

DoctorSpecialties.name: Pediatrics
DoctorSpecialties.description: Medical care for infants, children, and adolescents

medicalHistory.title: Lisinopril 10mg
medicalHistory.description: Take once daily for blood pressure control

Tasks.title: Prep for Knee Surgery
Tasks.notes: Ensure patient is fasting and gown is ready

Tasks.notes: Review insulin dosage and dietary habits
